scope: Reduce structural runtime cost in the repo-local 515-domain portfolio harness so a full-universe interactive rerun can complete in-session

decision_context.prior_state: The repo-local portfolio wrapper was correct but too slow to finish interactively, even after lowering persona counts and bootstrap resamples.
decision_context.decision: Fix the two measured structural hotspots: repeated full-edge scans during persona expertise assignment and repeated full-signal awareness recomputation during simulation rounds.
decision_context.why_now: Without runtime fixes, the trusted 515-domain rerun could not complete today and the wrapper would remain operationally unusable for interactive research loops.

files_expected.purpose: Adds node-level edge indexing so graph edge lookup is no longer a repeated full-list scan.
Adds signal indexing and round-local awareness caching so simulation avoids repeated signal scans and duplicated awareness recomputation.
Summarizes the sampled hotspots, the fixes, and the measured small-harness improvement.
Captures the request, profiling evidence, and verification plan for the runtime-fix tranche.

planned_steps: Profile a tiny full-universe portfolio run to identify structural hotspots
Replace graph edge full-scans with adjacency lookup
Index signals by domain once per round and cache effective awareness within a round
Rerun the tiny full-universe benchmark to confirm the runtime shape improves
Commit the execution-surface fix before relaunching the 515-domain readout run

commands: $env:PYTHONPATH='src'; python -c "from chip_labs.mirofish.portfolio import run_full_portfolio_evaluation; import time; t=time.time(); run_full_portfolio_evaluation(max_rounds=2, flagship_count_per_type=1, ensemble_runs=1, ensemble_count_per_type=1, min_runs=1, bootstrap_resamples=5); print(round(time.time()-t,2))"
expected_checks: The tiny full-universe portfolio run finishes in interactive time instead of effectively stalling
The sampled hotspot moves away from persona edge scans and repeated awareness recomputation
Trend and portfolio regression tests still pass

guardrails: Do not change domain ranking logic or adoption semantics in this tranche
Do not alter the 515-domain universe itself
Keep the changes as execution-surface optimizations rather than methodology mutations
